section.title: Expectations
section.p: MTH 254 is a technically demanding course that asks you to develop your spatial understanding, particularly in three dimensional mathematics, and often with change happening over time (so, four-dimensional). We will practice and develop your writing and visualization skills regularly. These take time for me to review, assess, and give you feedback. So it is important that you complete and turn in writing assignments on time.

The ideas are covered in a natural sequence, and if you stay on top of the work load, you will succeed. Topics will be discussed in class, with exercises to solidify your understanding outside of class.

At this level of learning, students are responsible for having awareness of their own understanding. I need you to come to me with questions (in class or outside of class) when there is something we are covering that you are struggling to understand or apply. Or if not to me, to a tutor or a peer.

p.alert: The workload includes reading the assigned sections of our textbook.

section.p:
Here are some other expectations:
Play an active role in your learning. Don't expect that it's enough to come to class and listen. You have to read the assigned sections, do the assigned homework, and seek out help when it's needed.
Do not use phones, tablets, or computers during class time unless it is directly related to a class activity.
Complete homework assignments several days earlier than their due date. Begin working on homework assignments the same day that we discuss that material in class.
Check your pcc.edu email at least once a day.
Food and drink are distracting to other students. Please do not bring food and drink to class. (Beverages that do not smell strongly and are in spill-proof containers are OK.)
